inputs.label: Age
inputs.businessContext: Age affects basal metabolic rate (BMR) due to changes in lean body mass and hormonal factors.

businessContext_i18n.en: Age affects basal metabolic rate (BMR) due to changes in lean body mass and hormonal factors.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Gender influences BMR through differences in body composition and hormonal profiles.

businessContext_i18n.en: Gender influences BMR through differences in body composition and hormonal profiles.

inputs.label: Weight
inputs.businessContext: Body weight is a primary driver of energy expenditure and calorie needs.

businessContext_i18n.en: Body weight is a primary driver of energy expenditure and calorie needs.

inputs.label: Height
inputs.businessContext: Height is used in BMR equations to estimate lean body mass and metabolic rate.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height is used in BMR equations to estimate lean body mass and metabolic rate.

inputs.label: Activity Level
inputs.businessContext: Physical activity multiplier adjusts TDEE based on occupational and exercise habits.

businessContext_i18n.en: Physical activity multiplier adjusts TDEE based on occupational and exercise habits.

inputs.label: Weight Loss Rate
inputs.businessContext: Desired weekly weight loss rate determines the daily calorie deficit target.

businessContext_i18n.en: Desired weekly weight loss rate determines the daily calorie deficit target.

inputs.label: Body Fat Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Optional input for more accurate BMR estimation using the Katch-McArdle formula.

businessContext_i18n.en: Optional input for more accurate BMR estimation using the Katch-McArdle formula.

inputs.label: Pregnant
inputs.businessContext: Pregnancy increases energy requirements; deficit is not recommended during pregnancy.

businessContext_i18n.en: Pregnancy increases energy requirements; deficit is not recommended during pregnancy.

inputs.label: Lactating
inputs.businessContext: Lactation adds ~500 kcal/day to energy needs; deficit must be adjusted.

businessContext_i18n.en: Lactation adds ~500 kcal/day to energy needs; deficit must be adjusted.

rules.action: set goalRate to 'slow' and warn user that deficit is not recommended during pregnancy
add 500 kcal to TDEE before calculating deficit
fallback to Mifflin-St Jeor BMR formula
ensure deficit <= 1000 kcal/day and warn if weight < 60 kg

thresholds.alert: Daily deficit exceeds 1000 kcal. Risk of muscle loss, nutrient deficiency, and metabolic slowdown.
Calorie intake is below minimum safe levels. Consult a healthcare professional.
BMI indicates underweight. Weight loss is not recommended.
